paper_title: Experimental Procedures for PSII and Protein Purification in Marine Diatoms

methods.description: Process for preparing oxygen-evolving PSII from a marine centric diatom, C. gracilis, involving cell disruption, centrifugation, and purification steps to obtain stable PSII particles for further experiments.

steps.description: Cells suspended in a medium containing 1 M betaine, 50 mM MES-NaOH (pH 6.5), and 5 mM MgCl2 were disrupted by freeze-thawing and then incubated in the presence of DNase I and 1 mM PMSF for 30 min at 0°C in the dark.
steps.searchable_summary: Disrupt cells using freeze-thawing and incubate with DNase I and PMSF

steps.description: Supernatants after centrifugation at 3,000 x g for 3 min were centrifuged at 40,000 x g for 10 min, and its precipitates (thylakoid membranes) were suspended in a medium containing 1 M betaine, 50 mM MES-NaOH (pH 6.5), and 1 mM EDTA (buffer A).
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge to obtain thylakoid membranes and suspend in buffer A

steps.description: Thylakoid membranes were treated with 1% Triton X-100 in buffer A at 1 mg of chlorophyll (Chl)/ml for 5 min at 0°C in the dark and then fractionated by differential centrifugations.
steps.searchable_summary: Treat thylakoid membranes with Triton X-100 and fractionate

steps.description: Crude PSII was solubilized with 1% Triton X-100 for 20 min at 0°C in the dark and applied to a DEAE-Toyopearl 650 M column equilibrated with buffer A containing 0.03% Triton X-100.
steps.searchable_summary: Solubilize crude PSII and apply to DEAE-Toyopearl column

steps.description: Purified PSII was concentrated by centrifugation at 40,000 x g for 20 min after addition of 10% PEG 6000, suspended in buffer B, and stored at -196°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Concentrate and store purified PSII

methods.method_name: Purification Procedures of Five Extrinsic Proteins
methods.description: Method for purifying five extrinsic proteins (PsbO, PsbQЈ, PsbV, Psb31, and PsbU) from crude PSII using various buffer treatments and chromatography techniques.

steps.description: Crude PSII suspended in 10 mM MES-NaOH (pH 6.5) (buffer C) was treated with 1 M Tris-HCl (pH 8.5) at 0.5 mg of Chl/ml for 1 h on ice in the dark to release all of the extrinsic proteins.
steps.searchable_summary: Treat crude PSII with Tris-HCl to release extrinsic proteins

steps.description: Treated samples were centrifuged at 227,000 x g for 30 min after addition of 14% PEG 1450. The supernatants were dialyzed using Spectra/Por dialysis membrane against buffer C.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge and dialyze to purify extrinsic proteins

steps.description: Dialyzed samples were applied to a DEAE-Toyopearl 650 M column (pH 6.5) equilibrated with buffer C, and then the fraction containing PsbO and PsbV was eluted at 0.2 M NaCl.
steps.searchable_summary: Elute PsbO and PsbV using DEAE-Toyopearl column

steps.description: The fraction passed through the DEAE column was applied to a CM-Toyopearl 650 M column (pH 6.5) equilibrated with buffer C, and Psb31 was then eluted at 0.2 M NaCl.
steps.searchable_summary: Elute Psb31 using CM-Toyopearl column

steps.description: The fraction passed through the CM column (pH 6.5) was dialyzed against 10 mM citric acid/NaOH (pH 5.5) and applied to another CM-Toyopearl 650 M column (pH 5.5) equilibrated with the same buffer. PsbQЈ was eluted at 0.1 M NaCl.
steps.searchable_summary: Elute PsbQЈ using CM-Toyopearl column

steps.description: The fraction passed through the CM column was dialyzed against 10 mM citric acid/NaOH (pH 4) and applied to a CM-Toyopearl 650 M column (pH 4) equilibrated with the same buffer. PsbU was eluted at 1 M NaCl.
steps.searchable_summary: Elute PsbU using CM-Toyopearl column

methods.description: Procedure to dissociate extrinsic proteins from purified PSII using various chemical treatments and analyze the resulting polypeptides.

steps.description: Purified PSII was treated either with 1 M NaCl, 1 M MgCl2, 1 M Tris-HCl (pH 8.5), 2.6 M urea plus 0.2 M NaCl or 4 M urea plus 0.2 M NaCl at 0.25 mg of Chl/ml for 20 min on ice in the dark.
steps.searchable_summary: Treat PSII with chemical solutions to dissociate proteins

steps.description: Treated samples were centrifuged at 40,000 x g for 10 min after addition of 10% PEG 6000. The polypeptides of the resulting precipitates and supernatants were analyzed by SDS-PAGE.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge and analyze polypeptides by SDS-PAGE

methods.description: Method for analyzing polypeptides using SDS-PAGE to separate proteins based on their molecular weight.

steps.description: Samples were solubilized with 5% lithium lauryl sulfate and 75 mM dithiothreitol for 30 min at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: Solubilize samples with lithium lauryl sulfate and dithiothreitol

steps.description: Solubilized samples (1 g of Chl) were applied to a gradient gel containing 16-22% acrylamide and 7.5 M urea.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply solubilized samples to gradient gel

steps.description: After electrophoresis at a constant current of 8 mA for 15 h, gels were stained with Coomassie Brilliant Blue R-250 and photographed.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform electrophoresis and stain gels

methods.description: Experiments to reconstitute purified PSII with extrinsic proteins under specific conditions to study protein interactions and functionality.

steps.description: Purified PSII was treated with 4 M urea plus 0.2 M NaCl to remove all of the extrinsic proteins.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove extrinsic proteins from PSII using urea and NaCl

steps.description: Urea/NaCl-treated PSII was incubated with the extrinsic proteins in various combinations for 20 min on ice at 0.05 mg of Chl/ml.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate PSII with extrinsic proteins

steps.description: Reconstitution experiments were performed in buffer B containing 10 M 2,6-dichloroindophenol, 2 mM MnCl2, 10 mM MgCl2, and 10 mM CaCl2 on ice at room light.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform reconstitution in buffer B under light conditions

steps.description: After reconstitution, the mixtures were centrifuged at 40,000 x g for 5 min after addition of 10% PEG 6000, and the resulting precipitates were suspended in buffer B.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge and suspend reconstituted PSII in buffer B

methods.description: Assays to estimate the relative amounts of PsbV and PsbU proteins rebound by reconstitution experiments using antibodies and chemiluminescent detection.

steps.description: Proteins on the SDS-polyacrylamide gel were transferred onto a PVDF membrane and reacted with respective antibodies.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer proteins to PVDF membrane and react with antibodies

steps.description: Horseradish peroxidase (HRP)-conjugated anti-IgG was used as a secondary antibody.
steps.searchable_summary: Use HRP-conjugated anti-IgG as secondary antibody

steps.description: Chemiluminescent detection of HRP was carried out using ECL Western blotting detection reagents and an ECL camera system.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform chemiluminescent detection using ECL reagents

steps.description: The peak area corresponding to each band was calculated with the NIH image software (ImageJ).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate band peak areas using ImageJ

methods.description: Measurement of oxygen evolution activity using a Clark-type electrode to assess the functionality of reconstituted PSII.

steps.description: Oxygen evolution was measured with a Clark-type electrode at 25°C in buffer B with 0.4 mM phenyl-p-benzoquinone as the electron acceptor.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure oxygen evolution using Clark-type electrode
